steps.title: Remove the parentheses
steps.content:
Rewrite the expression without parentheses while keeping every term in the same order.
$(7nr^2+4nr)+(5n^2r+6nr)=7nr^2+4nr+5n^2r+6nr$

steps.title: Identify the like terms
steps.content: Only $4nr$ and $6nr$ have the exact same variable part, so only those terms can combine. The terms $7nr^2$ and $5n^2r$ stay separate because their exponents are different.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Add the coefficients on the matching $nr$ terms.
$4nr+6nr=10nr$
The expression becomes $7nr^2+5n^2r+10nr$. The answer is D.
